ocr_mistral.text:
SINGLE AFFIDAVIT
FOR GENERAL PURPOSES IN UNITED STATES CLAIM BUSINESS.

A NEW RULE.

The Department of Washington have made a new rule, requiring which a witness came his or her name to an affidavit by making his or her mark (X). Has two persons who can write must also a such signature by signing to the left side of the name of the affiant making a mark. See "The Officer administering the oath can not be one of the attesting parties. Thus, are thereable:

Attest:
JOHN BROWN.
JOSEPH SMITH.

Elwin A. subscribed, Jr.
HENRY H. HART.
AMOS RIPLEY,
Justice of Peace, Ar.

State of Kentucky County of Henderson SS.

On this 24th day of August A.D. 1867, before me H. S. Park, a Notary Public within and for the County and State aforesaid, personally came and appeared Peyton Bibb aged 28 years, a resident of the City of Henderson in the State of Kentucky, who, being by me duly sworn according to law, on oath declares that "Was late 1st Sergt Co. "G" in the Use Hundred & Eighteenth Regiment United States Colored Infantry and was acquainted with Aaron Chambers decd Late a private in Same Company & Regiment who died in the United States service in the State of Virginia on the Sixth day of Nov 1864 from the Effect of Dearborn which he incurred in the service aforesaid under the following circumstances that he had the dearer had very bad in company quarter and we were ordered to March on day above mentioned and he was sent to field Hospital and died before reaching Hospital that he was a man of sound physical when he entered the army and a good soldier a man of steady & sober habits. That I have no interest in this matter.

P. H. A. H.
Satt Sergt Co. "G"
110th U.S.C.T. Inf.

Sworn to and subscribed before me, this 27th day of August in the year 1867, and I hereby certify that the affiant is a respectable and credible person and resides as stated; that I believe his statements are correct and worthy of full faith and credit; that I have no interest in this matter. I further certify that the foregoing affidavit was read over, fully explained to, and understood by the affiant before the signing and execution thereof, and also that the affiant is the identical party represented as making the affidavit.

H. S. Park
N. P. H. Co. Ky

transcribe_handwriting.text:
State of Kentucky     County of Henderson    SS.

On this 27th day of August A.D. 1867, before me, Al L. Park
personally appeared D. H. Hill a person whom I certify [is] credible and whom
I know to be the identical person he represents himself, 
who being duly sworn according to law, 
on oath declares that he is lately discharged private of Co.
G, 110 U. S. Colored Inf[antry]. In the Kebuck(?) Hospital
while under treatment for pleurisy he contracted
Hemiplegia from defective exudation [unclear] or extensive [unclear]
[unclear] he was transferred [unclear] muster [unclear] service
in the Unassigned Craft. Mr. Carty deposes
that his illness, debility or partial paralysis
is due to exposure and hardships incident
to a [unclear] in which he was applied [unclear]
that he was amiably dispensed with and [unclear]
[unclear] fairly as before expressed.
[Unclear] in Co. G, 110 Reg. USCI in good
health.
